Here's how it works.

Our skin is a patchwork of cells.

Up to age 30, old skin cells get broken down and recycled so that younger, healthier cells can take their place.

Yes, that's correct.

But as we get older, this process breaks down.

The skin then suffers a buildup of old, decaying cells that refuse to die.

They're called senescent cells.
